# Skilly

> Skilly is a macOS menu-bar AI tutor that watches your screen, hears your voice, answers out loud, and physically points at the right UI element with a live transcript floating beside the cursor. Works with every app on your Mac.

## Core pages

- [Homepage](https://tryskilly.app/): product overview, features, pricing, FAQ
- [Privacy policy](https://tryskilly.app/privacy): data handling, PostHog, OpenAI, WorkOS
- [Terms](https://tryskilly.app/terms)
- [Download (GitHub release)](https://github.com/tryskilly/skilly/releases/latest/download/Skilly.dmg)
- [Machine-readable pricing](https://tryskilly.app/pricing.md)

## Pricing

- Beta: $19 / month (monthly billing only during beta)
- 3 hours of live AI teaching per month
- 15 minutes free trial on first launch — no credit card required
- Cancel anytime — access continues through end of billing period
- Beta limited to 50 users, beta price locked for life on beta accounts

## What Skilly does

You hold Control+Option and ask a question out loud (or enable Live Tutor for hands-free voice activation). Skilly captures your screen via macOS ScreenCaptureKit, streams audio and screen context to OpenAI Realtime, receives a spoken answer plus UI element coordinates, and animates a blue cursor across your screen to the exact button, menu, or slider you need — while a live transcript of the spoken answer streams as text beside the cursor. Works on any application.

## Skills (bundled as examples)

Skilly works with any macOS app out of the box. Skills are optional expansion packs that add two things:

1. **Fresh knowledge** from the app's official documentation, beyond the AI's training cutoff
2. **Structured curriculum** with stages, completion signals, and UI vocabulary — so Skilly can coach you through a course, not just answer one-off questions

Five skills ship as examples:

- Blender Fundamentals — 6 stages, from navigation to first render
- After Effects Basics — compositions, layers, keyframes, effects
- Premiere Pro Basics — timeline editing, transitions, export
- DaVinci Resolve Basics — Cut, Edit, Color, Fairlight, Deliver pages
- Figma Basics — frames, components, auto-layout, prototyping

A skill builder is on the roadmap so anyone can create a skill for any app or workflow.

## How it works

1. Open any macOS app (Excel, Notion, browser, design tool — anything)
2. Hold Control+Option and ask out loud — or turn on Live Tutor and just start talking
3. Skilly answers out loud, streams a live transcript beside the cursor, and points at the exact UI element you need

## Tech stack

- macOS menu-bar-only app (LSUIElement)
- Built on OpenAI Realtime API (gpt-realtime model, single WebSocket)
- macOS native APIs: ScreenCaptureKit for screen, AVAudioEngine for audio
- WorkOS AuthKit for sign-in
- Polar for billing and subscription management
- Open-source Cloudflare Worker proxy — no API keys ship in the binary
- Sparkle for signed auto-updates

## Privacy posture

- Audio and screens stream only during a live teaching session and are not persisted afterward
- OpenAI is contractually blocked from training on Skilly session content
- Analytics (PostHog) capture only anonymous metrics — never transcripts, screenshots, or prompts
- Website uses PostHog in `persistence: 'memory'` mode with no cookies and no localStorage identifiers
- Auth tokens stored in macOS Keychain with `kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly`

## Languages and voices

- 16 languages supported with manual selection or speech-based auto-detection
- 8 AI voices selectable

## Platform availability

- macOS 14+ on Apple Silicon and Intel (available)
- Windows, Linux, iOS/iPad — on the waitlist, not yet available

## Built by

Skilly is built by Mohamed Saleh Zaied (@tryskilly on X, eng.mohamedszaied on GitHub), an iOS/macOS engineer who got tired of Googling "how do I X in this app?" fifty times a week while learning creative software.
